CREATE TABLE http_requests (id INT, method VARCHAR(10), protocol VARCHAR(10), query_string VARCHAR(255), PRIMARY KEY (id));
INSERT INTO http_requests (id, method, protocol, query_string) VALUES (1, 'GET', 'HTTP/1.1', 'name=value&other=value');
INSERT INTO http_requests (id, method, protocol, query_string) VALUES (2, 'POST', 'HTTP/2.0', 'id=123');
INSERT INTO http_requests (id, method, protocol, query_string) VALUES (3, 'PUT', 'HTTP/1.0', '');
INSERT INTO http_requests (id, method, protocol, query_string) VALUES (4, 'DELETE', 'HTTP/1.1', 'test=value');
SELECT method, protocol FROM http_requests WHERE id = 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM http_requests WHERE method = 'GET' AND protocol = 'HTTP/1.1';
SELECT * FROM http_requests WHERE query_string IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM http_requests WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM http_requests WHERE method = 'POST');
SELECT method, COUNT(*) AS request_count FROM http_requests GROUP BY method HAVING request_count > 1;
SELECT * FROM http_requests WHERE query_string LIKE '%value%';
UPDATE http_requests SET protocol = 'HTTP/3.0' WHERE id = 2;
DELETE FROM http_requests WHERE method = 'DELETE' AND protocol = 'HTTP/1.1';
SELECT method, protocol FROM http_requests WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM http_requests);
CREATE TABLE users (id INT, username VARCHAR(50), PRIMARY KEY (id));
INSERT INTO users (id, username) VALUES (1, 'user1');
INSERT INTO users (id, username) VALUES (2, 'user2');
SELECT u.username, r.method FROM users u JOIN http_requests r ON u.id = r.id WHERE r.method = 'GET';
SELECT DISTINCT r.method FROM http_requests r WHERE r.protocol = 'HTTP/1.1' AND r.id > (SELECT AVG(id) FROM http_requests);
CREATE INDEX idx_method ON http_requests (method);
CREATE INDEX idx_protocol ON http_requests (protocol);
